data.title: Short-term and Low-dose Application of Neuromodulators Increases Treatment Efficacy and Minimizes Antidepressant Discontinuation Syndrome in Refractory Functional Dyspepsia
data.brief_summary: The study was designed to investigate the effect of central neuromodulators on refractory functional dyspepsia
endpoints: Changes from baseline Leeds Dyspepsia Questionnaire (LDQ) scores at 2 weeks and 4 weeks were obtained to assess the dyspepsia outcomes.
Changes from baseline Patient Health Questionaire-9 (PHQ-9) scores at 2 weeks and 4 weeks was obtained to assess the depression contition after treatment.
Changes from baseline Generalized Anxiety Questonaire-7 (GAD-7) scores at 2 weeks and 4 weeks were obtained to assess the anxiety contition after treatment.
